title: A につけ B につけ
structure: Phrase A (dictionary/adjective form) + につけ、Phrase B (dictionary/adjective form) + につけ
structureEn: Phrase A (dictionary/adjective form) + につけ、Phrase B (dictionary/adjective form) + につけ
explanation: Expresses 'whether... or...', 'both... and...', 'in either case'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～AにつけBにつけ pattern is used to show that some state or action applies in both situation A and situation B. It often pairs contrasting or related conditions and can be translated as 'whether A or B', 'in either case', etc.

title: A のやら B のやら
titleEn: Used when the speaker is unsure or confused between multiple possibilities.
structure: Noun1 + のやら + Noun2 + のやら (also works with adjectives and verbs + のやら)
structureEn: Noun1 + のやら + Noun2 + のやら (also works with adjectives and verbs + のやら)
explanation: Used when the speaker is unsure or confused between multiple possibilities. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The A のやら B のやら grammar expresses the speaker's confusion or inability to decide between possibility A and B. It can be translated roughly as 'whether it's A or B (I don't know).'

title: Noun1 が Noun1 なら、 Noun2 も Noun2 だ
titleEn: Indicates a parallel or matching situation; 'if Noun1 is (truly) Noun1, then Noun2 is also Noun2'.
structure: Noun1 + が + (repeat Noun1) + なら、Noun2 + も + (repeat Noun2) + だ
structureEn: Noun1 + が + (repeat Noun1) + なら、Noun2 + も + (repeat Noun2) + だ
explanation: Indicates a parallel or matching situation; 'if Noun1 is (truly) Noun1, then Noun2 is also Noun2'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: This structure conveys a comparison: 'If Noun1 is indeed that kind of thing (Noun1), then naturally Noun2 is also that kind of thing (Noun2)'. It’s often used to show parallels or equivalences in a rhetorical way.

title: Noun1 も Noun1 なら、Noun2 も Noun2 だ
titleEn: Highlights two parallel facts or characteristics; 'if it’s said that ~, then it can also be said that ~'.
structure: Noun1 + も + (repeat Noun1) + なら、Noun2 + も + (repeat Noun2) + だ
structureEn: Noun1 + も + (repeat Noun1) + なら、Noun2 + も + (repeat Noun2) + だ
explanation: Highlights two parallel facts or characteristics; 'if it’s said that ~, then it can also be said that ~'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: This grammar point sets two items or facts side by side, emphasizing that both follow a similar logic or share a parallel characteristic. Often it can be rendered as 'If you say Noun1 is Noun1, then Noun2 is also Noun2.'

title: Noun + あっての + Noun
titleEn: Expresses 'B cannot exist without A'; 'B only exists because of A'.
explanation: Expresses 'B cannot exist without A'; 'B only exists because of A'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The 'Noun + あっての + Noun' pattern emphasizes that the second noun (B) exists only thanks to, or on the foundation of, the first noun (A). It's often translated as 'B is nothing without A' or 'B exists only because of A'.

title: Noun かたがた
titleEn: Do one thing while also doing another; 'while', 'and at the same time'.
explanation: Do one thing while also doing another; 'while', 'and at the same time'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: 「Noun かたがた」 is a somewhat formal expression for doing one thing while also doing another. The first action is often the main purpose, and the second is done simultaneously or incidentally.

title: Noun がてら
titleEn: Means 'while...at the same time', 'on the occasion of', or 'along with'.
explanation: Means 'while...at the same time', 'on the occasion of', or 'along with'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The suffix ～がてら is used to show doing one activity alongside another, taking advantage of the opportunity. It can often be translated as 'while', 'at the same time', 'along with', or 'on the occasion of'.

title: Noun からある
titleEn: Used with large numbers or measurements to emphasize 'at least that many' or 'no less than.'
explanation: Used with large numbers or measurements to emphasize 'at least that many' or 'no less than.' (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: 「Noun + Counter + からある」indicates a large or minimum number, emphasizing that the quantity is at least that high (often more). It conveys the idea that the figure is notably large or impressive.

title: Noun からする
titleEn: Used to indicate that something is judged, estimated, or considered from a certain point or aspect.
explanation: Used to indicate that something is judged, estimated, or considered from a certain point or aspect. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: 「Noun + からする」expresses a judgment or estimation based on a particular perspective or aspect. It translates to 'judging from', 'based on', or 'from the viewpoint of' in English, allowing the speaker to form an opinion or conclusion from that point.

title: Noun からの
titleEn: Defines the starting point or the origin; 'from'.
explanation: Defines the starting point or the origin; 'from'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: 「Noun + からの」indicates the place, source, or origin of something. It simply means 'from (Noun)'.
